flashcards.front: What is the difference between user-level threads and kernel-level threads?
flashcards.back: User-level threads are managed by a library in user space without kernel support, while kernel-level threads are supported and managed directly by the operating system kernel.

flashcards.front: Name the three primary multithreading models.
flashcards.back: 1. Many-to-One Model, 2. One-to-One Model, 3. Many-to-Many Model
Thread-local storage allows each thread to have its own copy of data, preventing sharing of certain variables between threads while maintaining the same variable name.

flashcards.front: What is the main advantage of the Many-to-Many threading model?
flashcards.back: It allows developers to create as many user threads as necessary while the operating system can create a sufficient number of kernel threads to support them, providing both flexibility and efficiency.

flashcards.front: What are the two types of thread cancellation?
flashcards.back: 1. Asynchronous cancellation: Immediately terminates the target thread. 2. Deferred cancellation: Allows the target thread to periodically check if it should cancel.
